title: Tower of Ranks
description: Window functions: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, cumulative SUM, LAG, LEAD — and ranking within partitions.

theory_scroll.content: Window functions compute a result per row based on a set of related rows — without collapsing the DataFrame like groupBy does. They are the most powerful tool in a Data Engineer's SQL arsenal.

rows: Unique sequential int — no ties ever
Same rank for ties; SKIPS next rank(s)
Same rank for ties; NEVER skips
Divide partition into k equal buckets
Value n rows BEFORE current row
Value n rows AFTER current row
First/last value in partition or frame

theory_scroll.content: WindowSpec anatomy: Window.partitionBy('col').orderBy('col').rowsBetween(start, end). partitionBy is like GROUP BY but keeps all rows. orderBy is required for ranking and offset functions. rowsBetween defines the frame for aggregates.
